title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions on this synthesis map are MODERN country borders. Season 3's origin regions span Indigenous Americas (pre-Columbian), Central Asian Scythian territories, Sumerian Mesopotamia, Inca Andes, Huichol desert sacred territory, and Ethiopian highland empires — none of which align with today's nation-states.

Origin 1 — Tobacco (Americas)
description: Tobacco's ORIGIN — Indigenous Americas. Taíno, Powhatan, Aztec, Maya all used tobacco ceremonially for 6,000+ years before Columbus.

title: Origin 2 — Cannabis (Central Asia / Hindu Kush)
description: Cannabis's ORIGIN — Hindu Kush + Pamir mountains in modern Afghanistan/Pakistan/Tajikistan. Scythian archaeological evidence of ritual use ~2,500 years ago.

title: Origin 3 — Opioids (Mesopotamia)
description: Opium's ORIGIN — Sumerian Mesopotamia, modern Iraq. 'Hul gil' (joy plant) documented in clay tablets ~3,400 BCE.

title: Origin 4 — Coca (Andean highlands)
description: Coca's ORIGIN — Quechua + Aymara Andes. 8,000+ years of Indigenous use. Spanish colonial exploitation at Potosí silver mines. 1860 cocaine isolation. 1886 Coca-Cola.

title: Origin 5 — Psychedelics (peyote — Chihuahuan Desert)
description: Peyote's ORIGIN — Wixárika (Huichol) sacred territory in the Chihuahuan Desert. Cannot be grown outside this specific ecosystem. Wirikuta UNESCO site anchor.

title: Origin 6 — Khat (Ethiopia)
description: Khat's ORIGIN — Ethiopian highlands. Harar + Oromia regions cultivated 1,000+ years. Daily economic anchor across the Horn of Africa + Yemen.

title: USA — global driver of prohibition policy (1971–present)
description: Modern political border. 1971 Nixon 'War on Drugs' declaration shapes global prohibition. Disproportionate enforcement devastated Black + Latino communities — cited throughout Season 3. Recent state-level reforms (Oregon, Colorado, California) challenge this model.

Portugal (modern borders) — 2001 decriminalization model
description: Modern political border. 2001 decriminalized possession of all drugs; funded treatment expansion instead of prisons. Overdose deaths and HIV rates among the lowest in Europe. Cited globally as successful alternative.
